free_text: The O-16(He3,a)O-15 and the O-16(He3,He3)O-16 reactions
The 68.5 cm variable energy cyclotron.
The accelerator produces an external 3He beam of up to
0.03 mu-A with energy between 6.00 and 11.00 MeV.
For scattering angles between 0 and 30 degr. a
Buechner-Bainbridge type magnetic spectrometer was
used.
For the range of angles 20-174 degr. an array
of three surface barrier counters, mounted about 15
degrees apart in a common rotating support, was used.
On each angular range the individual data points were
normalized by comparison with a fixed monitor detector.
The relative normalization of the two ranges was
obtained by comparison of points in the range 20-30
degr.
The absolute cross sections have an
uncertainty due mainly to uncertainties in target
composition and integrator calibration.
Data digitizing error
The alpha particles were detected by a 16 mm
long * 6 mm wide three surface barrier detectors,
indicating both position and energy, which was placed
at a fixed position in the focal plane of the
spectrometer.
A target of 0.60 mg/cm2 Mylar (C10H8O4) was used. The
thickness of the targets was measured by observing the
energy loss in the foils of a particles from a Po210
radioactive source. The target thickness was measured
with an uncertainty of about 5 %.

Error from the graphic bars
Fig.3
No explanation of relative units is given.
A target of NiO of 0.35 mg/cm2 thickness was used.
The thickness of  the targets was measured by
observing the energy loss  in the foils of a
particles from a Po210 radioactive  source. The
target thickness to be measured with an  uncertainty
of about 5 %. The NiO target was prepared  by heating
a thin nickel foil in oxygen at a pressure  of about
0.5 atm.
